page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the t2d server family. It is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC 7B13 processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, due to the low core count, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is a notable constraint, with PassMark memory scores in the bottom 10% and uncached read/write memory bandwidth benchmarks categorized as weak.

Given its hardware profile, the t2d-standard-2 is suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic text processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and low-concurrency web serving. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded compilation, or parallelized computing tasks due to its weak multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth. This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without demanding high memory throughput or local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. However, its multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations are relatively weak, with PassMark memory scores falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the t2d-standard-2 a cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale utility workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory throughput or parallel processing capabilities.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, optimized for lightweight single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual machine offering strong single-threaded execution and balanced memory for lightweight, entry-level general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
